data.body:
=== RESEARCH REPORT ===
=== DSO extraction (initial single-agent pass)
=== agent a3cbb49c1a179ff99

I now have a complete picture. I have read all three skill entry points fully, the routing/criteria docs, every reviewer prompt file with literal dimension language, the scenario red/blue team prompts, the UX probe set, the fidelity reviewers, the complexity-gate, the verifiable-sc-check, the complexity-evaluator (type/hierarchy routing), the feasibility-reviewer, the gap-analysis prompt, and the red-team-reviewer story_review mode. This is enough to produce the exhaustive, citation-backed inventory. Let me verify a couple of line numbers I'll cite for the brainstorm UX probe and the INVEST framework, then compile.

I have everything needed. Here is the exhaustive inventory.

---

# DSO Evaluation-Dimension Inventory

**Scope of audit.** Three skills under `~/digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/`: `skills/brainstorm/`, `skills/preplanning/`, `skills/implementation-plan/`, plus the standalone `skills/plan-review/` skill they share, and every reviewer prompt / agent / shared rubric they dispatch. All paths below are absolute under `/Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/`. "What it evaluates" describes the *judged property*; quotes are trimmed from the literal prompt text.

A structural fact worth stating up front: **the literal evaluation criteria almost never live in the SKILL.md files.** The SKILLs are orchestration narration; the actual judged dimensions live in `docs/reviewers/*.md`, `skills/shared/docs/reviewers/*.md`, and the dispatched agent files. Each reviewer file uses an identical 1–5 + N/A scoring scale and a two-column "What 4-or-5 looks like / What below-4 looks like" rubric table.

---

## GROUP 1 — BRAINSTORM (feature → epic spec)

Brainstorm's spec-level judging is done by (a) three **fidelity reviewers** (Agent Clarity, Scope, Value) that always run, (b) a **feasibility reviewer** (4th, conditional on integration signals), (c) a **scenario red-team / blue-team** pair, (d) inline **scrutiny-pipeline** self-review checks, (e) **UX probes**, and (f) the **complexity gate** on proposals. The pipeline that wires these is `skills/shared/workflows/epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md`.

### 1A. Fidelity reviewer — "Agent Clarity" (Senior Technical Program Manager)
Source: `skills/shared/docs/reviewers/agent-clarity.md`. Dispatched at `epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:289` (brainstorm Phase 2 Step 4, `SKILL.md:687`).

| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `self_contained` | Whether a planner could decompose the epic into stories with no clarifying questions — no undefined jargon/acronyms/undocumented-system references. | "A planner can decompose the spec into stories without asking clarifying questions… without requiring prior knowledge of the project." | agent-clarity.md:23 |
| `success_measurable` | Whether every SC is a specific, observable, pass/fail outcome — not subjective ("improved"/"better") and not effort ("implement the service"). | "Every success criterion describes a specific, observable outcome verifiable at the feature level… rather than 'Upload works'." | agent-clarity.md:24 |

Note the explicit carve-outs: post-sprint measurement SCs "must score 2 or below on `success_measurable`" (agent-clarity.md:28); do NOT penalize missing file paths/commands (agent-clarity.md:39).

### 1B. Fidelity reviewer — "Scope" (Senior Product Strategist)
Source: `skills/shared/docs/reviewers/scope.md`. Dispatched at `epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:290`.

| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `right_sized` | Whether the milestone/epic is one coherent deliverable — not an "epic-of-epics" and not a single trivial task. | "Too-large signals: (1) success criteria serve 2+ distinct user goals… Too-small signals: (1) the entire scope could be completed in one sprint by one developer — it's a task, not a milestone." | scope.md:23 |
| `no_overlap` | Whether scope duplicates deliverables/outcomes already claimed by another milestone in the roadmap. | "The success criteria duplicate deliverables already claimed by another milestone." | scope.md:24 |
| `dependency_aware` | Whether prerequisite milestones are named explicitly with reason. | "Any other milestones this one depends on… are named explicitly in the milestone spec, including why the dependency exists." | scope.md:25 |
| `consumer_completeness` | Whether all consumers of artifacts the epic touches (from Part C scan) are covered by an SC or explicitly descoped. | "All consumers of artifacts created or modified by this epic… are covered by the success criteria; any uncovered consumer is either explicitly descoped or addressed." | scope.md:26 |

### 1C. Fidelity reviewer — "Value" (Senior Product Manager)
Source: `skills/shared/docs/reviewers/value.md`. Dispatched at `epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:291`.

| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `user_impact` | Whether a specific user/stakeholder + their present-day problem is named, and SCs represent observable improvement (not "Refactor the service layer"). | "The Context narrative names a specific user or stakeholder affected, describes the problem they face today." | value.md:24 |
| `validation_signal` | Whether ≥1 SC gives an in-session-executable mechanism to confirm the capability solved the problem ("shipping is not the same as solving"). | "At least one success criterion includes a concrete mechanism for validating that the delivered capability addresses the user need… The primary validation signal must be executable within the sprint session." | value.md:25 |

### 1D. Feasibility reviewer (Senior Integration Engineer) — conditional 4th
Source: `agents/feasibility-reviewer.md`. Dispatched at `epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:292,309` only when integration signals present (`SKILL.md:650`, "feasibility skipped" exemption at `SKILL.md:687`).

| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `technical_feasibility` | Confidence the proposed external integrations actually work as the spec assumes, backed by docs + working GitHub examples. | "Is there verifiable evidence that this integration works the way the spec assumes it does?" / "All integration signals verified — official docs + working GitHub examples found for each." | feasibility-reviewer.md:10, 139 |
| `integration_risk` | Risk that integration failures block/delay implementation; higher score = lower risk. | "What is the risk that integration failures will block or significantly delay implementation?" | feasibility-reviewer.md:147 |
| (sub-check) CLI command-surface match | For CLI tools, per-subcommand `--help` verification that assumed flags/payloads match reality. | "Flag any mismatch between what the epic assumes and what `--help` reports as a critical capability gap — even if the mismatch is small (e.g., `--label` vs `--labels`)." | feasibility-reviewer.md:65 |
| (sub-check) auth HTTPS precondition | Whether deployment env supports HTTPS for OAuth/OIDC callbacks. | "An HTTP-only environment is a Contradicted signal for any OAuth callback flow." | feasibility-reviewer.md:115 |

### 1E. Scenario Red-Team (epic-level failure scenarios)
Source: `skills/brainstorm/prompts/scenario-red-team.md`. Run at `epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:248`, brainstorm `SKILL.md:649` ("when ≥3 SCs"). Each is a *generation* dimension (find failure scenarios in this category):

| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| Runtime scenarios | Timeouts, race conditions, out-of-order ops, concurrent modifications under real load. | "Timeouts: External calls… that exceed time limits under load… Race conditions: Concurrent operations that interleave in unexpected ways." | scenario-red-team.md:17-24 |
| Deployment scenarios | Migration/config conflicts, first-time setup gaps, env divergence, CI/CD regressions. | "Conflicts: Schema migrations, config key renames… First-time setup: Missing initialization steps." | scenario-red-team.md:27-34 |
| Configuration scenarios | Semantically-wrong-but-valid config, out-of-bound values, missing defaults, boundary values. | "Misuse: Configurations that are syntactically valid but semantically incorrect." | scenario-red-team.md:37-42 |
| Assumption (inferred premises) | Unstated hidden preconditions, taken-for-granted user behavior, unproven causal/architectural assumptions. | "Hidden preconditions: assumptions about system state or environment that are never stated (e.g., 'the cache is always warm')." | scenario-red-team.md:45-58 |

### 1F. Scenario Blue-Team (filter on red-team output)
Source: `skills/brainstorm/prompts/scenario-blue-team.md`. Run at `epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:250`. Four pass/fail gates a scenario must survive:

| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| Possible | Achievable given codebase/design. | "Reject scenarios that: Describe failure modes that cannot occur given the stated technical approach." | scenario-blue-team.md:21-26 |
| Actionable | A concrete remediation exists. | "Reject scenarios that: Are vague warnings without a clear fix." | scenario-blue-team.md:28-32 |
| Distinct | Not already covered by gap analysis / epic description. | "Reject scenarios that: Duplicate an artifact gap… already surfaced." | scenario-blue-team.md:34-39 |
| High Confidence | Evidence-based, not speculative on undecided implementation. | "Reject scenarios that: Assume a specific technical approach the epic deliberately leaves open." | scenario-blue-team.md:41-46 |

### 1G. UX probes (interaction quality for UI epics)
Source: `skills/brainstorm/prompts/ux-probe-set.md`. Fired at brainstorm Phase 1 Gate Step 1.5 (`SKILL.md:271-273`) only when UI intent detected.

| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `interaction_criticality` | Which interactions are highest-stakes if they fail. | "Which user interactions in this feature are highest-stakes — where a mistake, error, or delay would be most disruptive?" | ux-probe-set.md:13 |
| `non_happy_path_coverage` | What happens on validation errors, timeouts, empty states, partial data. | "What happens when things go wrong in this flow — validation errors, timeouts, empty states, or partial data?" | ux-probe-set.md:18 |
| `flow_entry_exit` | Where users enter and exit the flow. | "Where do users enter this flow (entry points) and where do they end up after completing or abandoning it?" | ux-probe-set.md:24 |

### 1H. Inline scrutiny-pipeline self-review checks (orchestrator-run, no sub-agent)
Source: `skills/shared/workflows/epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md`. These are judged *by the brainstorm orchestrator itself* during Step 1:

| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| Artifact contradiction (Part A) | User-named artifacts (files, CLIs, endpoints, config keys) all covered by an SC. | "Identify any artifacts… that the user explicitly named… but that are absent from or not covered by the success criteria." | epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:32 |
| Technical-approach self-review (Part B) | Sync loops, race conditions, invalidated assumptions, parsing ambiguities, deprecated-path references. | "Are there any sync loops?… Are there race conditions?… Does the approach invalidate existing assumptions?" | epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:58-63 |
| Inference-signal scan (Part B) | Inferred/unverified input sources flagged via hedging + confident-assertion trigger words, then MUST-execute Read/Grep to verify. | "scan… for trigger words that indicate an inferred input source — one that the implementation assumes will exist but has not been explicitly confirmed." | epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:65-84 |
| Shared-artifact impact (Part C) | Consumers of any moved/renamed/removed artifact, or call sites of a newly-prohibited behavior, covered or descoped. | "covered_by_SC: true if the consumer appears in any SC… flag them as potential scope gaps." | epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:88-115 |
| Web-research warrant (Step 2) | Whether 6 bright-line conditions (external integration, unfamiliar dependency, security/auth, novel pattern, perf/scalability, migration) require research. | "Research is always triggered when any of the following conditions apply." | epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:165-175 |
| Prompt alignment (Step 5) | When the epic modifies LLM-facing instructions, behavioral soundness via bot-psychologist. | "skill file modifications… agent definitions… prompt templates… hook behavioral logic." | epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:358-366 |

### 1I. Brainstorm SC-shaping rules (orchestrator self-applies to each drafted SC)
These are in the brainstorm SKILL.md and `verifiable-sc-check.md`. They are judging criteria on the *spec being authored*:

| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| Session-verifiability of SC | SC verdict must be renderable in the closing session (not days/weeks of telemetry). | "Session-infeasible SCs are prohibited from the verifiable SC list." | verifiable-sc-check.md:5; SKILL.md:551-552 |
| End-state-only litmus | SC must be a durable property, not a one-time transition ("OAuth has been migrated" → rejected). | "Could this item be false before the sprint began and true only because of this sprint's specific work? If yes, route to Closure Checks." | verifiable-sc-check.md:51 |
| Verify-intent completeness | Each SC's Verify-intent must contain subject + action + observable. | "Reject intents missing any element: 'The feature works correctly' — REJECTED." | verifiable-sc-check.md:78-85 |
| Outcomes-not-outputs | SC describes what users see/do, not what code does. | "Describe outcomes, not implementation ('Users can download results as CSV' not 'Implement CSV export endpoint')." | SKILL.md:553 |
| Executable-artifact end-to-end | CI/Actions/deploy artifacts need ≥1 SC exercising them end-to-end. | "include at least one SC that exercises the artifact end-to-end." | SKILL.md:555 |
| Scope-bullet concreteness | Every Scope bullet names a concrete in/out decision (no "verify whether", "TBD"). | "A bullet is invalid if it contains any of these patterns: 'verify whether', 'check if', 'TBD'." | SKILL.md:244 |
| State-lifecycle ownership | Any shared state has named CREATE/UPDATE/CONSUME/RETIRE owners. | "RETIRE must always be filled in… an unowned RETIRE row is itself a state-lifecycle defect." | SKILL.md:587 |

---

## GROUP 2 — PREPLANNING (epic → stories)

Story-level judging is done by a **6-reviewer Architecture Board** (`pass_threshold: 4`), plus the **story-decomposer** constructive checks, the **red-team/blue-team** adversarial pass (Phase E), the lightweight **Risk & Scope Scan** (Phase C), and **INVEST** (Phase F). Routing doc: `skills/preplanning/docs/review-criteria.md`.

### 2A. Architecture Board — 6 reviewers (one per concern), `pass_threshold: 4`
Routing: `review-criteria.md:19-26`. All 14 dimensions below must score ≥4 (or null).

**Security** — `skills/preplanning/docs/reviewers/security.md`
| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `access_classification` | Every new endpoint/data path declares public/authenticated/internal — the failure is *ambiguity*, not absent auth. | "The key failure is ambiguity, not that auth is absent — a clearly documented public endpoint scores well; an undeclared endpoint scores poorly." | security.md:23 |
| `data_protection` | Sensitive data encrypted in transit/at rest; PII handled per policy; no secret-logging. | "Sensitive data is encrypted at rest and in transit; PII/secrets are identified and handled per policy." | security.md:24 |

**Performance** — `skills/preplanning/docs/reviewers/performance.md`
| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `latency` | Time-bounded outcomes; avoid synchronous blocking on hot paths. | "Done definitions include observable time-bounded outcomes (e.g., 'within 30 seconds')." | performance.md:24 |
| `resource_efficiency` | Avoid N+1 queries, redundant API/LLM calls, unbounded memory. | "Story scope avoids N+1 query patterns, redundant API calls, and unbounded memory growth." | performance.md:25 |
| `scalability` | Input-size boundaries + concurrent-access patterns; or explicit single-user scope. | "Story defines behavior at input size boundaries… concurrent access patterns are addressed." | performance.md:26 |

**Accessibility** — `skills/preplanning/docs/reviewers/accessibility.md`
| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `wcag_compliance` | WCAG 2.1 AA addressed; keyboard/screen-reader/contrast outcomes in DDs. | "done definitions include observable accessibility outcomes (keyboard navigable, screen-reader compatible, sufficient contrast)." | accessibility.md:24 |
| `inclusive_ux` | Reduced motion, keyboard-only, screen-reader, touch targets, no color-only meaning. | "Story scope accounts for reduced motion, keyboard-only users, and screen reader users." | accessibility.md:25 |

**Testing** — `skills/preplanning/docs/reviewers/testing.md`
| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `user_journey_coverage` | Both success AND failure experiences defined (incl. backend caller-facing contract on error/timeout). | "Done definitions describe what the user sees when things go wrong… not just the happy path." | testing.md:25 |
| `boundary_scenarios` | Non-obvious behaviors/input extremes (back button, long strings, oversized files, empties). | "Story considers realistic but non-obvious user behaviors and input extremes." | testing.md:26 |
| `verifiable_outcomes` | Observable measurable outcomes, not "works correctly"/"handles errors gracefully". | "'user sees an error message with the failed file name' rather than 'errors are handled correctly'." | testing.md:27 |

**Reliability** — `skills/preplanning/docs/reviewers/reliability.md`
| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `error_handling` | New failure points have circuit breakers/retry/graceful degradation. | "Story scope addresses how new failure points are handled: circuit breakers, retry logic with backoff, or graceful degradation." | reliability.md:23 |
| `failover` | Recovery without data loss/corruption; idempotency for writes. | "system can resume a failed operation without data loss or corruption; partial-progress state is durable or safely discarded." | reliability.md:24 |
| (modifier) blast_radius | Tiebreaker that lowers (never raises) a score by impact width. | "Use blast radius as a tiebreaker, not an override… Blast radius never raises a score." | reliability.md:46-53 |

**Maintainability** — `skills/preplanning/docs/reviewers/maintainability.md`
| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `coupling_risk` | New cross-component dependencies acknowledged + justified/mitigated. | "Story introduces new dependencies between previously independent components with no acknowledgment." | maintainability.md:24 |
| `changeability` | Likely-to-evolve rules made configurable, not hardcoded. | "Business rules that may change are scoped as configurable rather than hardcoded." | maintainability.md:25 |
| `documentation` | Non-obvious decisions / new patterns get a doc-update DD (ADR/CLAUDE.md/DESIGN.md). | "Story done definitions include updating relevant documentation for non-obvious decisions." | maintainability.md:26 |

### 2B. Story-decomposer constructive checks (opus, dispatched in Story Decomposition phase)
Source: `agents/story-decomposer.md` (referenced at preplanning `SKILL.md:356`); the orchestrator validation re-checks these at `SKILL.md:445-451`.

| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| SC coverage plan | Every epic SC mapped to ≥1 story; each gets verdict (fully/partial/uncovered/out-of-scope). | "sc_coverage_plan is an array with one entry per SC passed in." | preplanning/SKILL.md:447 |
| DD → SC traceability | Each DD ends with `← Satisfies: sc-N` (or `← Validates Closure Check`). | "done_definitions (each ending with `← Satisfies: sc-N`)." | preplanning/SKILL.md:448 |
| No dangling deps | Every draft's `depends_on` references an existing or in-batch story. | "no dangling references." | preplanning/SKILL.md:450 |

### 2C. SC-contradiction check (orchestrator self-applies to each DD)
Source: preplanning `SKILL.md:878-891`.

| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| SC contradiction | A DD whose achievement would still leave its SC unsatisfied (bypass annotation, partial coverage, scope narrowing). | "A done definition contradicts its SC when the DD's observable outcome, if fully achieved, would leave the SC unsatisfied." | preplanning/SKILL.md:880 |

### 2D. DD-quality micro-checks (orchestrator, per DD)
| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| TDD Done-of-Done | Code-change stories must include "unit tests written and passing" as a DD. | "Code-change stories… must include 'unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic' as a Done Definition." | preplanning/SKILL.md:895 |
| DD lexicon (compound) | DD must not join two requirements with " AND ". | "A compound violation… contains ` AND ` joining two requirements — split into two." | preplanning/SKILL.md:916 |
| DD lexicon (vague) | DD must not use "properly"/"correctly"/"should". | "A vague violation… uses imprecise language (e.g., `properly`, `correctly`, `should`)." | preplanning/SKILL.md:916 |
| Spec-fidelity | PIL criteria must reappear in DDs (≥80% word match), no drops/mutations. | "A PIL criterion is considered matched when ≥80% of its words appear in any DD line." | preplanning/SKILL.md:938 |
| Scoping-assumption verification | Each "OUT: … already exists" claim must be confirmed by a `Verify:` command. | "For each OUT statement that makes a factual claim… write a `Verify:` command that confirms the assertion." | preplanning/SKILL.md:1304-1310 |

### 2E. Phase C Risk & Scope Scan (lightweight, no sub-agent)
Source: preplanning `SKILL.md:483-529`. Same six concern areas as 2A but as flag-raising not scoring (Security, Performance, Accessibility, Testing, Reliability, Maintainability) — `SKILL.md:489-496`. Plus:
| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| Split-candidate detection | Stories where walking-skeleton minimum and ideal diverge significantly. | "stories where the minimum functional goal (walking skeleton) and the ideal implementation diverge significantly." | preplanning/SKILL.md:523 |

### 2F. Phase E Adversarial Review — red-team `story_review` mode
Source: `agents/red-team-reviewer.md` (dispatched at preplanning `SKILL.md:563`). The **mandatory SC→DD coverage audit** plus a 7-category interaction-gap taxonomy:

| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| SC→DD coverage audit | Each epic SC classified fully/partially/uncovered/out-of-scope against collective story DDs. | "A story DD 'covers' an SC only when all three are true: produces the same observable outcome… scope matches or exceeds… measurable in the same terms." | red-team-reviewer.md:164-171 |
| Implicit shared state | Stories read/write same state with no explicit dependency. | "Two stories that assume exclusive ownership of the same data model or UI surface area." | red-team-reviewer.md:190-195 |
| Conflicting assumptions | Incompatible assumptions about behavior/flows/formats. | "One story assumes a field is optional while another treats it as required." | red-team-reviewer.md:198-203 |
| Dependency gaps | Missing deps that break wrong-order execution. | "A story that consumes output from another story but has no declared dependency." | red-team-reviewer.md:206-210 |
| Scope overlaps | Ambiguous/overlapping boundaries → duplicate work or gaps. | "Two stories that both claim responsibility for the same functional area." | red-team-reviewer.md:213-217 |
| Ordering/sequencing | Implied temporal order not captured in deps. | "Stories that must be deployed in a specific order but have no dependency enforcing it." | red-team-reviewer.md:220-224 |
| Consumer impact / operational readiness | Modifying a consumed system without verifying consumers. | "A story that changes data format, API contract… without testing downstream consumers." | red-team-reviewer.md:227-232 |
| Residual references | Rename/relocate/remove without enumerating all callers. | "A story that renames a config key, file path, function, or API endpoint without enumerating all callers." | red-team-reviewer.md:235-240 |

Blue-team-filter (`agents/blue-team-filter.md`) then triages these — same possible/actionable/distinct/high-confidence shape as 1F.

### 2G. INVEST (Phase F) and vertical-slicing
Source: preplanning `SKILL.md:648-664`.
| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| INVEST | Each story Independent/Negotiable/Valuable/Estimable/Small(one sub-agent session)/Testable. | "Each story must satisfy INVEST — Independent, Negotiable, Valuable, Estimable, Small (one sub-agent session), Testable." | preplanning/SKILL.md:650 |
| Vertical slice | Story delivers a slice of value, not a horizontal layer. | "Focus on functional 'slices' of value, not horizontal technical layers." | preplanning/SKILL.md:654 |
| Foundation/Enhancement split | Whether min-viable and ideal diverge enough to split. | "Does the minimum that delivers the functional goal differ significantly from the ideal experience or architecture?" | preplanning/SKILL.md:670 |

---

## GROUP 3 — IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN (story → atomic TDD tasks)

Plan-level judging: an **architectural review** (Step 2, 3 reviewers, `pass_threshold: 4`) on new patterns, then a **5-reviewer plan committee** (Step 4, `pass_threshold: 5`), plus a **gap-analysis** opus pass (Step 6, COMPLEX only), plus **proposal-generation** distinctness/complexity gates (Step 1) and the **3-gate task granularity** the task-decomposer enforces. Routing: `skills/implementation-plan/docs/review-criteria.md`.

### 3A. Step 2 Architectural Review — 3 reviewers, `pass_threshold: 4`
Dispatched at `implementation-plan/SKILL.md:482-485`.

**Best Practices** — `skills/implementation-plan/docs/reviewers/architectural/best-practices.md`
| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `modern_standards` | No deprecated APIs / anti-patterns for the stack in use. | "would a framework maintainer reviewing this code flag anything as outdated?" | best-practices.md:24 |
| `simplicity` | Simplest design; no speculative abstraction; new helper only at 3+ consumers. | "New helpers, utilities, or base classes are only introduced when there are 3+ concrete consumers." | best-practices.md:25 |
| `testability` | Each component unit-testable in isolation; ≤2 collaborators to mock. | "A developer can write a test for any single component without mocking more than 2 collaborators." | best-practices.md:26 |

**Project Alignment** — `skills/implementation-plan/docs/reviewers/architectural/project-alignment.md`
| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `pattern_consistency` | Matches existing structural approach; honors 5 architectural invariants (ClientFactory, SharedState, OutputProcessor, no agent-node DB writes, DataService). | "the pattern does not introduce a parallel abstraction for something that already has one." | project-alignment.md:24 |
| `convention_compliance` | Correct directories/naming/signatures; config via BaseConfig; no new dep without justification. | "New files placed in correct directories… class names follow existing conventions." | project-alignment.md:25 |

**Justification** — `skills/implementation-plan/docs/reviewers/architectural/justification.md`
| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `divergence_justified` | A new pattern names the existing alternative, a concrete verifiable limitation, and a measurable advantage — "why not the existing pattern?" | "If it claims novelty, the claim is accurate — it is not the existing pattern repackaged with different names." | justification.md:24 |

### 3B. Step 4 Plan Committee — 5 reviewers, `pass_threshold: 5`
Routing: `review-criteria.md:19-25`. **Threshold is 5** — "safe for unsupervised agent execution" (`review-criteria.md:11`).

**Task Design** — `skills/implementation-plan/docs/reviewers/plan/task-design.md`
| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `atomicity` | Each task passes 3 gates: Testable Behavior, Codebase Green, Maximum Granularity. | "Gate 1 — Testable Behavior… Gate 2 — Codebase Green… Gate 3 — Maximum Granularity." | task-design.md:23 |
| `acceptance_criteria` | Universal (test/lint/format) + category-specific + `Verify:` command exit-0, no placeholders. | "each criterion has a `Verify:` command that returns exit 0 on pass." | task-design.md:24 |
| `verify_command_robustness` | 8 defect classes (substring grep, shell-expansion, wrong argv, fixture validity, cardinality guard, format coverage, sibling-file deps, prerequisite-state ordering). Never null. | "grep/awk patterns use word-boundary anchors… shell metacharacters are correctly escaped… ACs that assert a count include a cardinality guard." | task-design.md:25 |

**TDD** — `skills/implementation-plan/docs/reviewers/plan/tdd.md`
| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `tdd_discipline` | Each task names a specific failing test to write first. | "Every task names a specific failing test to write first." | tdd.md:80 |
| `test_isolation` | Tests independently runnable, no shared mutable state, proper mocking. | "Each task's specified test is independently runnable." | tdd.md:81 |
| `red_green_sequence` | Test demonstrably fails before implementation (RED→GREEN), not implement-then-test. | "write test → see it fail → implement → see it pass. Not: implement → write test that passes." | tdd.md:82 |
| `test_boundary_coverage` | Tests target the right abstraction; assert observable behavior, not internals. | "Tests assert on observable behavior… not on internal method calls or private state." | tdd.md:83 |
| `red_test_dependency` | Every RED test declared as a dependency edge before its impl task. | "no implementation task starts before its RED test exists." | tdd.md:84 |
| `exemption_justification` | Any TDD-exempt task cites a valid exemption criterion verbatim/by number. | "Every task that claims a TDD exemption… cites a specific valid criterion." | tdd.md:85 |
| `bidirectional_test_coverage` | Modify-test and remove-test tasks exist for changed/deleted behavior, not create-only. | "One-directional test coverage (create-only) is insufficient." | tdd.md:86 |
| (modifier) Instruction-file scoring | Non-executable `.md`/agent/prompt tasks scored on structural boundary tests, not behavioral. | "Do not demand behavioral correctness assertions… Such assertions are non-deterministic." | tdd.md:55-66 |

**Safety** — `skills/implementation-plan/docs/reviewers/plan/safety.md`
| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `incremental_deploy` | Migrations reversible; no cross-service coordinated deploy; destructive ops preceded by drain/deprecation. | "destructive operations… are preceded by a deprecation or drain step." | safety.md:24 |
| `backward_compat` | Expand-contract; nullable-before-enforce; versioned endpoints; no hard cutover. | "Expand-contract pattern is followed: new nullable fields before enforcement." | safety.md:25 |

**Dependencies** — `skills/implementation-plan/docs/reviewers/plan/dependencies.md`
| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `dag_validity` | All deps explicit; valid acyclic DAG; clear critical path; no implicit ordering. | "the dependency graph is a valid DAG with no cycles; the critical path is identifiable." | dependencies.md:23 |
| `no_coupling` | No two tasks share mutable state / write the same resource without coordination. | "No task shares mutable state with another task in a way that would cause race conditions." | dependencies.md:24 |

**Completeness** — `skills/implementation-plan/docs/reviewers/plan/completeness.md`
| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `criteria_coverage` | Every AC maps to ≥1 task; cleanup + doc tasks present. | "Every acceptance criterion in the story maps to at least one task." | completeness.md:23 |
| `e2e_coverage` | User-facing/API/cross-component flows each have an E2E test task in `tests/e2e/` or documented rationale. | "User-facing changes… each have a dedicated E2E test task in `tests/e2e/`." | completeness.md:24 |
| `ac_semantic_consistency` | Each `Verify:` command tests the entity the criterion names and is executable; migration ACs check removal AND replacement. | "if the criterion mentions entity X, the verify command references entity X, not entity Y." | completeness.md:25 |
| `dd_collective_ac_coverage` | Union of task ACs proves every story DD; each owning task has a `## Story DD Coverage` section; disjoint partition. Never null. | "the union of task ACs across the plan is sufficient to prove every DD without an additional human check." | completeness.md:26, 28-38 |

### 3C. Step 6 Gap-Analysis (opus, COMPLEX only)
Source: `skills/implementation-plan/prompts/gap-analysis.md`. Run at `implementation-plan/SKILL.md:80` (Step 6; "TRIVIAL: skipped"). 6-category taxonomy:

| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| Race conditions | Tasks read-then-write same state with no dependency / shared resource via parallel agents. | "Two tasks that read-then-write the same state without a declared dependency between them." | gap-analysis.md:27-32 |
| State & file conflicts | Tasks editing same file / DB table / config key / conflicting migrations. | "Multiple tasks editing the same source file." | gap-analysis.md:34-40 |
| Implicit assumptions | Task assumes another's output/import/config without declaring the dependency. | "A task that consumes another task's output but doesn't specify the expected format/schema." | gap-analysis.md:42-48 |
| Missing error/rollback paths | New failure modes without error handling/rollback/timeout. | "New API endpoints without error response handling." | gap-analysis.md:50-57 |
| Cross-task interference | A task's side effects break another's preconditions (cleanup removes dependency, rename invalidates path). | "A cleanup task that removes something a later task depends on." | gap-analysis.md:59-65 |
| AC verify-command defects | Same 8 verify-command defect classes as 3B `verify_command_robustness`. | "Substring false positives… Shell-expansion leakage… Wrong helper-script argv." | gap-analysis.md:67-78 |

### 3D. Step 1 Proposal generation gates (approach-proposer, opus)
Source: `implementation-plan/SKILL.md:368-378`, schema `prompts/proposal-schema.md`, gates `shared/prompts/complexity-gate.md`.
| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| Proposal distinctness | Every pair differs on ≥1 of 4 axes: data layer, control flow, dependency graph, interface boundary. | "every pair of proposals must differ on at least one of the four structural axes." | implementation-plan/SKILL.md:372-377 |
| Minimum count | ≥3 distinct proposals (≥2 only if genuinely constrained, documented). | "the agent must produce at least 3 distinct proposals." | implementation-plan/SKILL.md:370 |
| Complexity gates | Gate 1 YAGNI, Gate 2 Rule of Three, Gate 3 new-dependency recorded per proposal. | "every proposal is gated through Gate 1 (YAGNI), Gate 2 (Rule of Three), and Gate 3." | implementation-plan/SKILL.md:378 |

### 3E. Step 1 Unsatisfiable-criteria / ambiguity self-checks (orchestrator)
| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| Ambiguity scan | 6 ambiguity signals (undefined scope, implicit AC, conflicting signals, missing persona, unstated constraints, ambiguous priority). | table "Undefined scope boundaries… Missing persona… Unstated constraints." | implementation-plan/SKILL.md:290-298 |
| Unsatisfiable SC | SC contradicted by codebase / mutually exclusive / impossible without out-of-scope redesign → REPLAN_ESCALATE. | "SC contradicted by codebase state… SC items mutually exclusive… Architecture makes SC impossible." | implementation-plan/SKILL.md:303-308 |

### 3F. Task-decomposer 3-Gate granularity (the constructive criteria)
Source: `implementation-plan/SKILL.md:677-680`. (Mirrored by the Task Design `atomicity` reviewer in 3B — see overlap notes.)
| Gate | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|
| Gate 1 Testable Behavior | "A valid test executes the code and asserts on output, exit code, or side effects." | implementation-plan/SKILL.md:678 |
| Gate 2 Codebase Green | "after committing only this task, all tests pass and the system is deployable." | implementation-plan/SKILL.md:679 |
| Gate 3 Maximum Granularity | "it must not be possible to split into smaller tasks each meeting Gate 1 and Gate 2." | implementation-plan/SKILL.md:680 |

## GROUP 4 — PLAN-REVIEW (shared standalone gate; `pass_threshold` ≥4, single pass)

Source skill: `skills/plan-review/SKILL.md`; rubric prompt: `docs/workflows/prompts/plan-review-dispatch.md`. Invoked before ExitPlanMode / presenting a design or plan. **NOT used by brainstorm/preplanning/implementation-plan for their gates** — those three explicitly prohibit substituting it (brainstorm `SKILL.md:628-633, 689`). Four dimensions:

| Dim | What it evaluates | Literal language | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| Feasibility | Can it be built? Missing steps, impossible constraints, tools-exist, hidden deps. | "Can this actually be built as described?" | plan-review-dispatch.md:12-16 |
| Completeness | Error/edge cases, testing strategy, integration points. | "Are error cases and edge cases addressed where they matter?" | plan-review-dispatch.md:18-22 |
| YAGNI / Overengineering | Unnecessary abstractions, premature generalization, unrequested features; new config-key consolidation. | "Are there unnecessary abstractions or premature generalizations?" | plan-review-dispatch.md:24-29 |
| Codebase Alignment | Naming/file-org conventions, established patterns, accurate references. | "Does the plan match how this project actually works?" | plan-review-dispatch.md:31-35 |

## CONSOLIDATED CROSS-SKILL DIMENSION LIST — with overlaps marked

These are the distinct *judged concepts* that recur across skills. Joe asked for overlaps documented, not merged — each is still listed at its source above; this section is the cross-walk.

1. **Outcome-measurability of the spec/criteria** — `success_measurable` (agent-clarity.md:24) ≈ `verifiable_outcomes` (testing.md:27) ≈ brainstorm SC rules (SKILL.md:553, verifiable-sc-check.md) ≈ `criteria_coverage` measurability (completeness.md). Same idea at 3 altitudes: epic SC, story DD, task AC.
2. **Coverage/traceability up the hierarchy** — SC→DD audit (red-team-reviewer.md:164) ≈ story-decomposer `sc_coverage_plan` (preplanning/SKILL.md:447) ≈ SC-contradiction check (preplanning/SKILL.md:880) ≈ `dd_collective_ac_coverage` DD→AC (completeness.md:26) ≈ `criteria_coverage` (completeness.md:23). Deliberately redundant constructive-vs-adversarial pairs (decomposer constructs, red-team attacks — preplanning/SKILL.md:477).
3. **YAGNI / simplicity / no-speculative-abstraction** — complexity-gate Gates 1-3 (complexity-gate.md:26-59) ≈ `simplicity` (best-practices.md:25) ≈ `divergence_justified` (justification.md:24) ≈ plan-review `yagni` (plan-review-dispatch.md:24) ≈ brainstorm "YAGNI ruthlessly" (SKILL.md:520,806). Five separate encodings of one principle.
4. **Atomicity / one-concern / deployable-green** — 3-Gate granularity (implementation-plan/SKILL.md:677-680) **duplicated verbatim** in `atomicity` reviewer (task-design.md:23). Also relates to complexity-evaluator "single-concern check" (complexity-evaluator.md:246).
5. **Dependency-graph validity** — `dag_validity`/`no_coupling` (dependencies.md) ≈ gap-analysis race/state-conflict/implicit-assumption (gap-analysis.md:27-48) ≈ red-team dependency-gap/ordering (red-team-reviewer.md:206,220).
6. **Verify-command robustness** — `verify_command_robustness` (task-design.md:25) **near-duplicate** of `ac_semantic_consistency` (completeness.md:25) and gap-analysis category 6 (gap-analysis.md:67-78). Three reviewers check the same 8 defect classes.
7. **Coupling / cross-component dependency** — `coupling_risk` (maintainability.md:24) ≈ `pattern_consistency` invariants (project-alignment.md:24) ≈ `no_coupling` (dependencies.md:24) ≈ red-team implicit-shared-state (red-team-reviewer.md:190).
8. **Consumer/residual-reference completeness** — `consumer_completeness` (scope.md:26) ≈ scrutiny Part C (epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:88) ≈ red-team consumer-impact + residual-references (red-team-reviewer.md:227,235).
9. **Failure-path / error-handling / boundary coverage** — `error_handling`+`failover` (reliability.md) ≈ `user_journey_coverage`+`boundary_scenarios` (testing.md) ≈ scenario red-team runtime/config (scenario-red-team.md) ≈ gap-analysis missing-error-path (gap-analysis.md:50) ≈ UX `non_happy_path` (ux-probe-set.md:18). Strong overlap across all three skills.
10. **Deployment safety / backward compatibility** — `incremental_deploy`+`backward_compat` (safety.md) ≈ scenario deployment scenarios (scenario-red-team.md:27) ≈ brainstorm release-infra check (SKILL.md:345).
11. **Self-contained / no-clarifying-questions** — `self_contained` (agent-clarity.md:23) ≈ preplanning "Story-Level Fidelity" (SKILL.md:1294) ≈ plan committee "score of 5 = trust unsupervised agent" (review-criteria.md:44).
12. **External-integration feasibility** — feasibility-reviewer (whole agent) ≈ scrutiny Step 2 web-research triggers (epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:165) ≈ preplanning Phase D/G integration research ≈ complexity-evaluator `external_boundary_count` (complexity-evaluator.md:186).
13. **Documentation of non-obvious decisions** — `documentation` (maintainability.md:26) ≈ `criteria_coverage` doc tasks (completeness.md:23) ≈ preplanning doc-router (SKILL.md:970).

## TYPE / HIERARCHY COUPLING — every place DSO branches on ticket type or parent/child (for the children-existence + complexity generalization)

This is the most important section for the planned pivot. DSO couples behavior to type/hierarchy in these specific places:

**A. The whole skill chain is hierarchy-staged by type.** brainstorm = epics only ("No child tasks — this skill creates the epic only", brainstorm/SKILL.md:814); preplanning = epic→stories; implementation-plan = story→tasks. Each refuses or reroutes off-type input:
- brainstorm **Type Detection Gate**: `ticket_type == epic` proceeds; `story/task/bug` forces a convert-to-epic vs enrich-in-place choice (brainstorm/SKILL.md:83-112). → *Generalize:* this is "is this the top of a tree?" — replaceable by "does it have/There should be children?"
- preplanning **complexity routing** (SKILL.md:194-201): SIMPLE epic → skip to implementation-plan; MODERATE → lightweight; COMPLEX → full decomposition. → *This is already a complexity pivot, but keyed to `tier_schema=SIMPLE` epic vocabulary.*
- implementation-plan **Epic Type Detection** (SKILL.md:192-197): `type=epic` → "epic-direct mode" (tasks parented to epic, skip context-file check); else story mode. Pure type branch.

**B. complexity-evaluator hard-codes type into the rubric** (`agents/complexity-evaluator.md`):
- `tier_schema=SIMPLE` for epics vs `TRIVIAL` for stories — vocabulary swap keyed to level (complexity-evaluator.md:14-17).
- **scope_certainty rubric forks on `type: bug` vs `type: story/epic`** with different criteria tables (complexity-evaluator.md:118-154). "If the ticket `type` field is absent… treat scope_certainty as Low and classify COMPLEX" (line 116) — brittle type-dependence.
- **Epic-only qualitative overrides** (multiple personas, UI+backend, new migration, foundation/enhancement, external integration, >6 SC) apply *only when evaluating epics* (complexity-evaluator.md:224-237). "Do NOT apply these dimensions when evaluating stories or bugs."
- Done-definition check and single-concern check are also "Applicable when evaluating epics only" (complexity-evaluator.md:239-253). → *Generalize:* these overrides are really "this work is too big / multi-concern for one unit" — a complexity signal, not an epic-specific one.

**C. Reviewer thresholds differ by level, not by anything intrinsic.** Preplanning Board `pass_threshold: 4` (review-criteria.md:10); implementation-plan committee `pass_threshold: 5` (review-criteria.md:11, "safety-critical… safe for unsupervised agent execution"). Same reviewer-style rubric, stricter bar deeper in the tree.

**D. TDD-story creation forks on an LLM-inferred epic *type*** (preplanning/SKILL.md:1003-1009): user-facing epic → E2E test story; external-API epic → integration test story; internal-tooling epic → "internal epic exemption", no test story. → *Brittle: a free-text type inference drives whether a whole story is created.*

**E. Hierarchy invariants enforced structurally.** "Epic Deps Must Not Contain Children" (preplanning/SKILL.md:1275-1282) — links must go child→child, never epic→child; blocking links get promoted to comparable hierarchy level. implementation-plan re-invocation guard branches on child states (fresh/in_progress_hold/all_closed/replan_required/diff_plan, SKILL.md:178-190).

**F. Doc-only / instruction-file type-coupling.** Multiple reviewers special-case non-executable `.md`/agent/prompt files as `still-valid`/exempt (tdd.md:44-74; implementation-plan/SKILL.md:351-363, 721; complexity-evaluator.md:95 treats skill/prompt/doc files as 0 layers). This is file-*type* coupling rather than ticket-type, but equally a brittle special-case.

→ **Generalization guidance the inventory supports:** every epic-only override in (B) and the type branches in (A,D) reduce to two underlying signals DSO already computes — **complexity** (files/layers/interfaces/scope_certainty/blast-radius, complexity-evaluator.md Dimensions 1-6) and **decomposability** (single-concern, >6 SC, foundation/enhancement, multi-persona). "Does the ticket have children?" cleanly subsumes the brainstorm/preplanning/implementation-plan staging in (A) and the epic-vs-story threshold split in (C). The bug-vs-story scope_certainty fork (B) is the one place where *type* carries genuinely distinct semantics (a bug has a reproduction/fix-scope axis a story doesn't) — worth preserving as a complexity sub-signal rather than discarding.

---

## RIGIDITY SMELLS — preserve the lesson, drop the rigidity

1. **Hard numeric thresholds presented as bright lines.** Cross-cutting = "≥3 architectural layers" / "≥5 interfaces/classes" (implementation-plan/SKILL.md:334-335); complexity files ">3", layers "≥3" (complexity-evaluator.md:84-101); ">6 success criteria" forces COMPLEX (complexity-evaluator.md:237); SC count "≥7" / scenario survivors "≥10" force COMPLEX (preplanning/SKILL.md:188-190). These are un-tuned magic numbers applied identically to a 2-file CLI and a payments rewrite.
2. **`pass_threshold: 5` with "ALL dimensions must equal 5"** for plan review (review-criteria.md:56) — a single 4 anywhere across 15 dimensions fails the whole plan and forces revision cycles. Combined with "Do NOT inflate scores — a 4 with suggestions is more useful than a false 5" (every reviewer), this is internally contradictory: reviewers are told 4 is honest *and* that 4 fails. Heavyweight for trivial work.
3. **HARD-GATEs that forbid substitution and mandate full pipelines even when redundant.** brainstorm "SUBSTITUTIONS PROHIBITED… no bypass, annotation, or override" (SKILL.md:628-636, 689); "all five steps are mandatory" scrutiny checklist (SKILL.md:643). preplanning Story Decomposition "HALT… Do NOT fall back to inline drafting" (SKILL.md:455). Lots of dispatched sub-agents (3-reviewer arch + 5-reviewer plan + opus decomposer + opus gap-analysis) fire regardless of whether the work is one file.
4. **Multiple blocking tag-gates that HALT the skill.** preplanning has 4 sequential HALT gates (`scrutiny:pending`, `interaction:deferred`, `ui_probes:deferred`, preconditions-validator) before any work (SKILL.md:79-136); implementation-plan has `BLOCKED:scrutiny_pending` etc. (SKILL.md:100-105). A trivial change inherits the full ceremony.
5. **One-question-at-a-time HARD-GATE in brainstorm** (SKILL.md:198-200): forbids ever asking two questions in one message, "No exception exists for 'quick context checks' or efficiency arguments." Good for novel features, punishing for a well-understood small change.
6. **Mandatory minimum-3-proposals** for every story including doc-only-ish ones (implementation-plan/SKILL.md:370) — though a Doc-Only Skip Gate (SKILL.md:351) and `insufficient_solution_space` escape (SKILL.md:404) exist, the default is heavyweight.
7. **Architectural invariants hardcoded to a specific app's class names** (`ClientFactory`, `SharedState`, `OutputProcessor`, `DataService`, `BaseConfig` — project-alignment.md:24-25; pipeline layer names in complexity-evaluator.md:93). These reviewer prompts are not portable to a different codebase without rewriting the invariant list.
8. **Type-absence defaults to maximum rigor.** "If the ticket `type` field is absent… classify COMPLEX" (complexity-evaluator.md:116) and confidence-Medium → COMPLEX (complexity-evaluator.md:203). Defensive, but means any classification uncertainty escalates to the heaviest path.
9. **Provenance/marker bookkeeping woven into the gates.** PIL marker is a HARD-GATE for the `brainstorm:complete` tag (SKILL.md:731-733); preconditions records must run "even if it seems redundant" (SKILL.md:760). The judging is entangled with ceremony that has nothing to do with whether the plan is good.

The transferable lessons (worth keeping) are the rubric *content* — the two-column "what good vs bad looks like" dimension tables, the "outcomes not outputs" SC discipline, the SC→DD→AC traceability chain, the 3-gate atomicity definition, the verify-command defect taxonomy, and the constructive-vs-adversarial redundancy. The rigidity to shed is the fixed numeric thresholds, the all-must-be-5 gating, the no-substitution HARD-GATEs, the type-keyed special-casing, and the app-specific hardcoded invariants.
